title: UK FCA Client Categorisation Notice
content:
MERIDIAN WEALTH PARTNERS LIMITED - CLIENT CATEGORISATION NOTICE

This notice explains how Meridian Wealth Partners Limited ('the Firm') categorises clients in accordance with the Financial Conduct Authority's Conduct of Business Sourcebook (FCA COBS 3.5) and the retained provisions of MiFID II following the UK's departure from the European Union.

CLIENT CATEGORIES

Under FCA regulations, all clients must be classified into one of three categories: Retail Client, Professional Client, or Eligible Counterparty. This categorisation determines the level of regulatory protection you receive and affects how we conduct business with you.

RETAIL CLIENTS receive the highest level of protection under FCA rules. This includes detailed pre-contractual information, comprehensive suitability assessments, and enhanced complaint procedures. Retail clients benefit from access to the Financial Services Compensation Scheme (FSCS) up to £85,000 per person per firm for deposits and up to £85,000 for investment business claims. The majority of our private wealth clients fall within this category.

PROFESSIONAL CLIENTS are presumed to have greater market knowledge and experience. Professional clients include entities with called-up share capital exceeding €5 million, large undertakings meeting two of the following criteria: balance sheet total of €20 million, net turnover of €40 million, or own funds of €2 million. Individuals may qualify if they have carried out transactions of significant size (averaging 10 per quarter over four quarters) in relevant markets, work in the financial sector for at least one year in a professional position, or hold a financial instrument portfolio exceeding €500,000.

ELIGIBLE COUNTERPARTIES include investment firms, credit institutions, insurance companies, UCITS funds, pension funds, and certain government bodies. These entities receive minimal regulatory protection as they are considered to have sufficient expertise to protect their own interests.

CATEGORISATION PROCESS

Meridian Wealth Partners conducts thorough client assessments during onboarding, reviewing factors including investment experience, financial situation, knowledge of relevant markets, and professional qualifications. We apply a conservative approach, ensuring clients are not inappropriately categorised as Professional where Retail categorisation would be more suitable.

For clients with investible assets exceeding £500,000, we conduct enhanced due diligence to determine appropriate categorisation. Our assessment considers transaction frequency, portfolio complexity, and the client's understanding of associated risks.

RIGHT TO REQUEST DIFFERENT CATEGORISATION

Retail clients may request Professional categorisation to access a broader range of investment opportunities, though this reduces regulatory protection. Such requests require demonstration of adequate expertise and experience through our comprehensive assessment process, including evaluation of at least two transactions per quarter over the previous four quarters in relevant markets.

Professional clients may request Retail treatment to benefit from enhanced protection, which we will generally accommodate unless operational constraints prevent appropriate service delivery.

ONGOING REVIEW

We review client categorisations annually or when circumstances materially change. Changes affecting categorisation must be reported within 30 days. Our compliance team monitors categorisation appropriateness through quarterly reviews of client files and transaction patterns.

COMPLAINTS AND COMPENSATION

Retail clients may refer complaints to the Financial Ombudsman Service if dissatisfied with our response. Professional clients and Eligible Counterparties do not have automatic access to the Financial Ombudsman Service.

CONSENT AND ACKNOWLEDGEMENT

By continuing our relationship, you acknowledge understanding of your categorisation and associated protections. Should you wish to discuss your categorisation or request changes, please contact your relationship manager or our compliance department at compliance@meridianwealthpartners.co.uk.

This notice forms part of our client agreement and should be retained for your records.
